meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Debug Code Efficiently
content.seo_description: Master API integration to crush debugging bottlenecks. This intermediate guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to elevate your student projects.
content.answer_block: Debugging API failures requires isolating the request-response cycle. Use Antigravity to simulate edge cases and Claude Code to automate trace analysis. By systematically logging headers, status codes, and payload structures, you can pinpoint integration errors in minutes rather than hours, transforming frustration into architectural mastery.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, the coffee is cold, and your terminal is spitting out a wall of cryptic 500-series errors. You’ve been staring at the same API integration for three hours, and the documentation feels like it was written in another language. Every developer hits this wall; the difference between a student and a senior engineer is how they dismantle it.

Today, we stop guessing. We are going to treat your API integration like a surgical procedure. By combining the rapid prototyping power of Antigravity with the autonomous debugging capabilities of Claude Code, you will move from 'stuck' to 'shipped' in a single session. Let’s turn that error log into a roadmap.

steps.title: Isolate the Request Payload
steps.description: Before debugging, ensure your data structure matches the API schema. We use Antigravity to generate a mock request that validates your JSON structure against the API's actual requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock JSON payload based on this OpenAPI spec [URL] and validate it against the required fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'validate payload structure against schema.json'

steps.title: Implement Structured Logging
steps.description: You cannot fix what you cannot see. We need to inject middleware that captures the full request/response cycle without cluttering your main logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python/Node.js middleware snippet that logs the full request headers and response body to a debug.log file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'add logging middleware to src/api_client.ts'

steps.title: Simulate API Failure States
steps.description: Your code must be resilient. We use Antigravity to simulate 401, 403, and 429 errors to see how your app handles failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock server configuration to return various HTTP error codes to test my error handling logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'test error handling with mock server output'

steps.title: Automated Trace Analysis
steps.description: Feed your logs into Claude Code to identify patterns in the failures that aren't immediately obvious to the human eye.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these log patterns and identify the common denominator in my failed API requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'analyze debug.log and explain why the requests are timing out'

steps.title: Refactor and Verify
steps.description: Apply the fix and verify that the integration is now stable under production-like conditions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the authentication handler to include a retry mechanism with exponential backoff.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'implement retry logic in api_client.ts'

pro_tips: Always wrap external API calls in a dedicated 'Adapter' pattern to decouple your business logic from the provider's quirks.
Use Postman collections to export cURL commands directly into your Antigravity prompts for perfect context.
Set up a 'circuit breaker' pattern if your API integration involves high-latency external dependencies.
Always check your environment variables in the logs; 80% of debugging is just finding a missing API key.
Treat your API documentation as an 'incomplete' source of truth; always trust the actual network traffic logs over the docs.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is stable, move on to building unit tests for your service layer to ensure that future changes don't break your hard-won progress.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow used by top-tier engineers to ship production-grade code. Stop struggling with errors and start building the features that will land you your first job. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
